frase_original: If he sees you in this apartment he will seriously murder you and then break up with me. And I really, really don’t want him to break up with me, Linc.”“But murdering me, that’s all good?

texto: She tugged the sleeves down over her hands, stretching the fabric until the seams reached her fingernails. Then she locked her fingers around them to ensure they stayed down.Veda fought the urge to rip those sleeves from Coco’s grip and force her to wear them appropriately, or at the very least roll them up so she wouldn’t be tempted to yank at them. She could remember a time when she’d had the same habit, back in middle school. As if hiding her hands behind a thin piece of fabric would protect her from the world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Trevion Burns: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “She tugged the sleeves down over her hands, stretching the fabric until the seams reached her fin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, bwwm, habits pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de habits e hiding.
